summary: Large naming/header cleanup for fighter part access. The PR renamed the Fighter bone/part array from fp->ft_bones to fp->parts, renamed the old _ftPart/ftPart enum to Fighter_Part, and prefixed part enumerators with FtPart_ across many fighter and item interaction sites. It also tightened ftParts_8007500C's declaration/asm signature from s32 parameters/return to Fighter_Part, added ft/forward.h includes where needed, and removed several non-MWERKS NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback stubs from ftparts.c. The value was mostly readability and type clarity: hard-coded part indices such as 0, 1, 4, 0x1F, 0x31 were replaced at many call sites with semantic names like FtPart_TopN, FtPart_TransN, FtPart_HipN, FtPart_LThumbNb, and FtPart_RThumbNb.
what_changed: Renamed the Fighter struct field at offset 0x5E8 from FighterBone* ft_bones to FighterBone* parts in src/melee/ft/types.h.
Renamed the part enum in src/melee/ft/forward.h from typedef enum _ftPart { TopN, TransN, ... } ftPart to typedef enum Fighter_Part { FtPart_TopN, FtPart_TransN, ... } Fighter_Part.
Updated many call sites from fp->ft_bones[...] to fp->parts[...] in core fighter code and character files.

Replaced many raw or unqualified part indices with FtPart_* enum names, e.g. ftParts_8007500C(fp, 0x31) became ftParts_8007500C(fp, FtPart_RThumbNb), and fp->ft_bones[TopN] became fp->parts[FtPart_TopN].
Changed ftParts_8007500C's header declaration and assembly signature to use Fighter_Part for both its part argument and return type instead of s32.
Added #include "ft/forward.h" in ftparts.c/ftparts.h and in several character files that now need the Fighter_Part enum names.

Removed several non-MWERKS #else NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback definitions in ftparts.c around assembly-only routines, including ftParts_80074B6C, ftParts_80074CA0, ftParts_80074D7C, ftParts_80074E58, ftParts_8007500C, ftParts_80075028, and ftParts_8007506C.
Updated comments that named old parts, such as changing 'adjust TopN' to 'adjust FtPart_TopN' in ftcoll.c/ftcoll.h.

Touched a broad set of fighter special-move and item setup paths where part joints are used for effects or item spawns, including Fox blaster/reflector/fire fox, Game & Watch items, Zelda specials, Pikachu specials, Yoshi shield, Samus charge shot, and others.
smart_moves: The PR body documents the rename as a scripted melee-replace-symbols batch, which is a good tactic for a repository-wide mechanical rename over 64 changed files.
Using the FtPart_ prefix prevents generic enum names like TopN, HeadN, HipN, and TransN from polluting the namespace and makes part-index arguments visually obvious at call sites.
Renaming fp->ft_bones to fp->parts aligns the field with the module name ftparts and with the semantic use as a fighter part table, not just raw bones.
Changing ftParts_8007500C to Fighter_Part documents that the function maps one fighter part enum value to another part/bone index, reducing ambiguity compared with s32.

Replacing literals in effect/item attachment code with part names makes attachment points reviewable: e.g. FtPart_TopN for root/top effects, FtPart_HipN for Fox reflector effects, FtPart_LHandNb and FtPart_RThumbNb for hand-held items.

The cleanup kept some unresolved character-specific numeric part indices where no shared FtPart_* semantic name was introduced, e.g. fp->parts[57] and fp->parts[78] in Captain/Ganon effect code, avoiding overclaiming names when evidence was not available.
decomp_lessons: For shared skeleton/part tables, prefer a namespaced enum type and namespaced enumerators before spreading usages across character code; this makes later decomp work easier to search and safer to review.
When a helper accepts a specific enum domain, update both the C prototype and any inline/asm signature to the domain type instead of leaving it as a raw integer.
Repository-wide symbol cleanup should be scripted and documented in the PR body so future reviewers can distinguish mechanical renames from behavioral changes.
Mechanical renames can require header dependency cleanup: files that only compiled with old generic enum names may need explicit ft/forward.h includes once the enum is made central.
Do not force semantic names for every index if the index is fighter-specific or not yet understood; retaining numeric indices is better than assigning a misleading generic part name.
Comments and item API prototypes should be updated alongside code so old identifiers like TopN or ft_bones do not remain as stale documentation.
naming_conventions: Use Fighter_Part as the enum type name for fighter parts.
Use FtPart_ as the prefix for fighter part enum values, e.g. FtPart_TopN, FtPart_TransN, FtPart_XRotN, FtPart_HipN, FtPart_HeadN, FtPart_LThumbNb, FtPart_RThumbNb, FtPart_TransN2.
Use Fighter.parts for the FighterBone* part array instead of Fighter.ft_bones.
Prefer fp->parts[FtPart_*].x0_jobj over fp->ft_bones[oldName].x0_jobj or fp->ft_bones[rawIndex].x0_jobj.
Prefer ftParts_8007500C(fp, FtPart_*) over ftParts_8007500C(fp, raw_integer) when the raw integer maps to a known shared part.
Update comments to use the new namespaced part names, e.g. FtPart_TopN instead of TopN.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The ftParts_8007500C assembly function signature was changed to asm Fighter_Part ftParts_8007500C(Fighter*, Fighter_Part), preserving the asm body while making the interface typed.

Several #else NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs were removed from MWERKS_GEKKO-guarded asm functions in ftparts.c; the diff does not include reviewer discussion, so the exact motivation is not stated beyond cleanup.

Some hunks also normalize constants in decompiled C, such as 0x48B to 1163, 0x18000 to 98304, DOUBLE_PI32 to 2 * (f32) M_PI, and 0.0f/1.0f to 0/1 in selected calls. The evidence suggests matching/style cleanup occurred alongside the part rename, but the PR title/body emphasize fighter parts.
The PR preserved known raw indices where the semantic part name was not apparent, which avoids accidental mismatch or false documentation.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining raw fp->parts[<number>] usages and decide whether they are character-specific or need named FtPart_* constants.
Search for remaining ft_bones references after this PR to catch stale names in comments, headers, or generated code.
Audit ftParts_8007500C call sites that still pass raw integers such as 1, 2, or 4 and replace with FtPart_* where evidence is clear.
Check item APIs in it_27CF.h and related files for parameters that should be typed as Fighter_Part instead of s32.
Review later PRs for continued use of the FtPart_ naming convention and whether more typed part helper signatures were introduced.
